contract_scope: Acceptance and ownership metadata only; this artifact does not implement fixes or alter product behavior.
common_ref_note: PRD/DESIGN refs are transcribed from the audit finding where present. Architecture refs name the governing implementation boundary that downstream fixes must preserve.

records.title: Search command shows Today/feed results instead of executing query

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: SQLite FTS5 lexical retrieval only
ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: HTTP and MCP expose the same product operations

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Verify Search surface communicates actual lexical query execution and never visually presents Today rows as query results.
acceptance_criteria: Submitting `search <query>` through Steer executes the lexical query immediately.
Result count and visible rows reflect the submitted query, not Today/default rows.
If only seeding the Search surface is intentionally retained, receipt explicitly says so and focuses submit.
records.evidence_requirement: Browser-level evidence plus API/contract evidence showing query, result count, and visible rows for a known matching term.

records.title: No-match Search returns internal error while still showing feed items

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: SQLite FTS5 lexical retrieval only

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Confirm lexical misses produce a stable empty state without stale/default content or generic internal error copy.
acceptance_criteria: Unique no-match query returns a no-results state.
Prior/default feed rows are cleared after empty or failed query.
No generic `err: internal: internal error` is shown for ordinary no-match retrieval.
records.evidence_requirement: Automated browser or contract test capturing a unique no-match query with empty-state text and zero unrelated rows.

records.title: Search surface has noisy/ambiguous interactive naming

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: browser SPA served by the single deployable

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Audit active accessibility tree so the Search submit control is unambiguous and result Inspect buttons do not collide with form submission naming.
acceptance_criteria: Search form submit has a distinct accessible name such as `submit search`.
Result-level Inspect controls remain clearly item-scoped.
Inactive/duplicate result controls are absent from active accessibility tree.
records.evidence_requirement: Accessibility snapshot or Playwright role queries demonstrating a unique Search submit and scoped result Inspect controls.

records.title: Natural-language Steer policy correction fails or gives no usable interpretation

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: OpenRouter JSON-in/JSON-out transformer validated by Go
ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: LLM never owns durable state or direct database writes

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Receipt must expose normalized interpretation and applied/specific rejection state without chatty onboarding or settings UI.
acceptance_criteria: Natural-language policy command shows `interpreted_as` or equivalent normalized rule.
Applied/rejected status is explicit.
Failure reason is specific and actionable, not a generic internal error.
records.evidence_requirement: Browser trace of the audited policy command showing normalized receipt or safe actionable rejection.

records.title: Add-feed via Steer works, but the receipt is too terse

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: one deployable; no onboarding/admin sidecar

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Keep receipt minimal but actionable, naming the source/title/host and orienting toward Source Ledger ingest without wizard/toast/settings expansion.
acceptance_criteria: RSS URL pasted into Steer still adds source.
Receipt includes source title or host/URL.
Receipt includes terse next action such as `run ingest in SOURCE LEDGER`.
records.evidence_requirement: Browser evidence of add-feed receipt and Source Ledger row after source addition.

records.title: Newly added feed can ingest and appear, but LLM understanding degrades

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: OpenRouter as sole LLM backend
ARCHITECTURE.md §2 Runtime OpenRouter LLM contract

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Feed/Inspector claims must not overstate summary provenance when model processing falls back or fails; /doctor remains the detailed diagnostic surface.
acceptance_criteria: Freshly ingested items distinguish model-backed, partial, and fallback states.
Fallback summaries/core insights are plainly labeled in scan/inspect paths.
Repeated provider/model failures are visible in `/doctor` without polluting normal reading copy.
records.evidence_requirement: Controlled source ingest plus /doctor and Inspector evidence showing aligned item provenance and model/fallback taxonomy.

records.title: Inspector still leaks real-source boilerplate and related-story tail

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: product behavior lives in internal/resofeed direct functions and SQL

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Inspector must remain a deliberate verification surface; readable body must exclude source furniture and related-story tails before display.
acceptance_criteria: Shared payload layer strips audited The Verge follow/topic prompt, taxonomy, and related-story tail patterns.
Inspector body for fixture article contains article payload without leaked source furniture.
Regression fixture covers the exact audited contamination pattern.
records.evidence_requirement: Regression test fixture plus browser Inspector evidence for the audited real-source tail pattern.

records.title: Resonate feedback is visually present but programmatically/semantically unclear

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: same product operations for humans and agents

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Preserve simple star visual while exposing durable active/inactive state programmatically.
acceptance_criteria: Resonate control exposes toggled state via aria-pressed/name/state attribute.
Accessible text or state changes after click.
Visual fill/background remains consistent with active state.
records.evidence_requirement: Accessibility snapshot before/after Resonate click showing state transition and visual screenshot evidence.

records.title: /doctor via Steer works, but direct /doctor route does not

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: one deployable serves static app and JSON HTTP
ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: thin transports share product operations

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Direct /doctor route must either show the diagnostic surface or visibly/documentedly redirect; URL must not render Today misleadingly.
acceptance_criteria: Navigating directly to `/doctor` shows diagnostics equivalent to Steer `/doctor`, or performs an explicit documented redirect.
URL and active surface semantics agree.
Today feed is not rendered as if it were the Doctor surface.
records.evidence_requirement: Browser navigation evidence for direct `/doctor` and Steer `/doctor` parity or explicit redirect behavior.

records.title: Mobile Source Ledger still leaves Today feed in the page/accessibility flow

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: browser SPA within one deployable

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: On narrow screens, non-feed surfaces must hide/unmount inactive feed content from layout and accessibility flow and move focus to active surface.
acceptance_criteria: Mobile Source Ledger excludes Today feed rows from visible and accessibility flow.
Focus moves to Source Ledger heading or back command.
Containment rule also applies to Search and Doctor where active.
records.evidence_requirement: Mobile viewport accessibility snapshot and screenshot for Source Ledger with no underlying Today content in active tree.

records.title: Mobile Search metadata still loses useful information

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: lexical retrieval only

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Search results on mobile need a dedicated metadata anatomy that preserves source, age, extraction, match, and provenance without single-line truncation.
acceptance_criteria: Mobile Search result metadata separates `src + age` from extraction/match/provenance.
Source names remain intelligible at audited viewport width.
Time group markers no longer consume the metadata line.
records.evidence_requirement: Mobile Search screenshot/DOM snapshot showing two-line or otherwise stable metadata layout.

records.title: Source error diagnostics need a full-view affordance

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: no separate dashboard/admin process

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Preserve flat ledger style while exposing full raw `err:` text through terse disclosure/title/details.
acceptance_criteria: Truncated source diagnostics expose full raw text by title/details/disclosure.
Ledger remains one-line/two-line flat style, not a dashboard.
Affordance is keyboard and assistive-tech reachable.
records.evidence_requirement: Ledger screenshot plus DOM/accessibility evidence of full diagnostic availability for a long error.

records.title: Time group labels compete with metadata on narrow/search rows

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: browser SPA within one deployable

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Search and narrow row layouts must prioritize source/provenance readability over inline time group markers.
acceptance_criteria: Time grouping is separated, minimized, or omitted where it undermines Search metadata.
Desktop Today row behavior remains acceptable.
Mobile Search source/provenance line is not displaced by `TODAY` marker.
records.evidence_requirement: Before/after mobile Search evidence showing time marker no longer competes with metadata.

records.title: Complex natural-language Steer still fails with internal error

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: OpenRouter JSON transformer validated by Go
ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: single owner token; actor_id is not authorization

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Realistic combined hide/boost sentences must receive applied partial receipt or specific safe rejection, never generic internal error.
acceptance_criteria: Complex hide+boost instruction produces normalized rule(s) or explicit safe rejection.
Partial application is stated when only part can be applied.
Generic internal error is absent from normal user receipt.
records.evidence_requirement: Browser evidence using the audited complex instruction and recorded normalized/applied/rejected receipt.

records.title: Simple natural-language Steer also fails

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: OpenRouter JSON transformer validated by Go

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Short natural-language steering requests must use the same translation, validation, and receipt path as longer requests.
acceptance_criteria: Simple reduce/hide instruction translates or rejects specifically.
Command does not remain ambiguously stuck in input after submission.
Receipt exposes normalized intent or actionable error.
records.evidence_requirement: Browser evidence using the audited simple instruction and resulting receipt.

records.title: Explicit hide/reduce intent did not affect Today

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: SQLite is durable source of truth; current state only

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Accepted steering policy must have visible Today ranking/filtering effect; failed steering must clearly state no policy was applied.
acceptance_criteria: Accepted hide/reduce rule visibly demotes or filters matching probe item in Today.
If rule fails, Today is unchanged only with explicit non-applied receipt.
Controlled corpus makes the effect deterministic enough to verify.
records.evidence_requirement: Controlled probe corpus test showing Today ordering/filtering before and after accepted hide/reduce steering.

records.title: Boost intent did not affect ranking against the probe corpus

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: SQLite state and direct product functions own ranking behavior

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Accepted boost rules must produce inspectable ordering changes in a small controlled corpus without adding ranking sliders/settings.
acceptance_criteria: Accepted boost rule ranks matching probe item above non-boosted comparator under controlled conditions.
Ranking explanation or metadata is inspectable enough to prove policy influence.
No settings/dashboard surface is introduced.
records.evidence_requirement: Controlled probe corpus test with accepted boost receipt and post-ingest Today ordering evidence.

records.title: LLM chain still reports model latency failures for newly ingested items

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §2 Runtime OpenRouter LLM contract
ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: OpenRouter sole LLM backend

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: /doctor must distinguish provider reachability from item-level transformation failures and make fresh item model state unambiguous.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh ingest with available OpenRouter reaches successful model state or reports specific item failure.
`resolved_model=unknown` is explained separately from provider reachability.
Model latency failures are not presented as healthy transformations.
records.evidence_requirement: Live or controlled OpenRouter-smoke evidence with redacted secrets plus /doctor item-state output.

records.title: Inspector is still fallback/excerpt-driven for probe items

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: OpenRouter JSON transformer; Go validates outputs

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Inspector must label excerpt/fallback summaries clearly and avoid presenting RSS excerpt as model-backed synthesis.
acceptance_criteria: Probe item with failed linked extraction/model transformation is labeled fallback/partial.
Summary/core insight provenance is visible in Inspector.
Excerpt-only text is not labeled as completed source-backed synthesis.
records.evidence_requirement: Probe Inspector screenshot/DOM plus /doctor evidence correlating extraction/model state with Inspector labels.

records.title: /doctor status semantics are misleading

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §2 Runtime OpenRouter LLM contract: account_default/resolved model semantics

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Keep raw diagnostic text but split provider reachability, model resolution, and item-transform health into stable scan-readable keys.
acceptance_criteria: /doctor no longer headlines `openrouter: ok` when item transformations are failing without context.
Output separately reports provider reachable/configured, model resolved/unresolved, and item transform failures.
Raw item diagnostics remain available below the summary keys.
records.evidence_requirement: /doctor output evidence before/after showing separated health keys and non-misleading headline semantics.

records.title: Feed rows omit visible quality/value tier metadata

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: current state stored in SQLite; no dashboard required

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Expose terse quality/value or equivalent priority microcopy in scan path without creating a dashboard/settings panel.
acceptance_criteria: Feed rows include objective quality/value tier or equivalent priority label.
Label is terse and legible within existing metadata grammar.
No new settings, dashboard, or archive concepts are introduced.
records.evidence_requirement: Desktop and mobile feed screenshots/DOM snapshots showing quality/value metadata on representative rows.

records.title: Inspector core insight and body still contain extraction pollution

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: OpenRouter is transformer; Go validates before saving/display

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Core insight and body must exclude source navigation, personalization copy, topic/author furniture, and cross-article contamination before presentation.
acceptance_criteria: GM/full-source-backed fixture does not include personalized-feed or unrelated leaked/cracked-item fragments.
Core insight is concise and not category/headline residue.
Fallback is labeled if clean insight cannot be produced.
records.evidence_requirement: Regression fixture and browser Inspector evidence for polluted core insight/body case.

records.title: Steer Enter-submit and surface navigation reliability need reproduction-grade coverage

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: one deployable browser SPA; thin transports

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Add browser-level coverage for Enter/apply submission and top-surface navigation from feed, Inspector, Search, and Ledger states; only fix behavior proven failing by that coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Coverage includes Steer Enter and apply click from feed, Inspector, Search, and Ledger.
Coverage includes TODAY and SOURCE LEDGER top-nav from open Inspector.
If failures are observed, active surface/input state is corrected and retested.
records.evidence_requirement: Reproduction-grade browser test results with screenshots/snapshots for each required state/action combination.

records.title: Mobile Search form consumes too much first-screen space

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: browser SPA within one deployable

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Mobile Search should default to query, submit, and result count with secondary filters compacted; retrieval must not feel like a settings dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: First mobile viewport shows query/submit/result count and at least part of results when available.
Source/date/resonated/limit controls are compacted or disclosed.
Lexical retrieval clarity is preserved.
records.evidence_requirement: Mobile Search screenshot at audited viewport showing reduced form footprint and visible results.

records.title: Add-feed receipt is too terse to orient the next action

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: no onboarding wizard/admin sidecar

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Same implementation owner as B5 is allowed, but UX acceptance remains a separate row: source identity plus next action, no wizard/toast stack/settings flow.
acceptance_criteria: Receipt reads like `source added: <title|host>; run ingest in SOURCE LEDGER` or equivalent.
Receipt remains terse and inline with Steer.
No onboarding wizard, toast stack, or settings flow is introduced.
records.evidence_requirement: Browser evidence of add-feed receipt demonstrating orientation copy separately cited for U2.

records.title: Source Ledger row grammar is still visually unstable

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: one SQLite DB/current state only

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Ledger rows should scan around predictable src/status/last_fetch/url/actions grammar; long URL and diagnostics remain accessible but not primary scan anchors.
acceptance_criteria: Rows expose predictable `src`, `status`, `last_fetch`, `url`, and actions grammar.
Long URLs do not destabilize primary scan anchor.
Raw diagnostics remain accessible through B12 affordance.
records.evidence_requirement: Desktop and mobile Source Ledger screenshots/DOM snapshots with long URL and missing-name cases.

records.title: Diagnostics should be more scan-readable without becoming a dashboard

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §2 Runtime OpenRouter LLM contract

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Keep /doctor raw monospace text, but split initial keys for provider_reachable, model_resolved, and item_transform_failures; do not add charts/badges/cards/remediation panels.
acceptance_criteria: First /doctor lines use stable scan-readable keys.
Raw item lines remain below summary keys.
No dashboard-style components are added.
records.evidence_requirement: /doctor screenshot/text capture showing stable keys and raw-line preservation.

records.title: Current surface state needs stronger programmatic semantics

architecture: ARCHITECTURE.md §1 Decisions: browser SPA; same product concepts for humans and agents

records.uiux_spec_audit_requirement: Every active surface needs heading, landmark/state, focus destination, and inactive-surface containment; top nav may use aria-current but Inspector/Search/Doctor also need route/surface semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Active Today, Inspector, Search, Doctor, and Source Ledger states are programmatically distinguishable.
Focus moves to active surface after command/navigation.
Inactive surfaces are hidden or contained from the active accessibility flow.
records.evidence_requirement: Accessibility snapshots across all surfaces proving active landmark/state, focus destination, and inactive containment.
